message:
test(merge): pin 3-way merge-change detection on relation-only edits via --no-auto-flush + explicit base snapshot

Two coupled changes to the 3-way merge reproduction test + rustfmt
noise on its neighbour file.

tests/repro_3way_merge_bug.rs — substantive:
  The reproduction was testing "local label changes should not be
  lost during merge when scalar content hash is unchanged on both
  sides", but it was doing two things that made the test fragile:

    1. It relied on the default auto-flush path, meaning every `br`
       invocation between the base snapshot and the simulated merge
       was potentially re-flushing the JSONL. On machines where
       auto-flush fires, the "local-only relation change" became a
       JSONL-visible change too, hiding the actual bug the test is
       meant to pin.
    2. It did not explicitly seed `.beads/beads.base.jsonl`; the
       test instead assumed `sync --flush-only` left a correct base
       snapshot. That assumption depends on implementation details
       that can change — and if the merge logic ever starts using a
       different base-snapshot path, the test would silently stop
       exercising the 3-way scenario.

  Fixes:
    - Read the freshly-synced JSONL and explicitly write
      `.beads/beads.base.jsonl` with the same bytes, so the 3-way
      merge has an unambiguous known base regardless of how the
      auto-flush / sidecar logic evolves.
    - Pass `--no-auto-flush` to the `br label add` invocation so
      the local label mutation genuinely stays out of the JSONL
      until the merge runs. Without this flag the label mutation
      was auto-flushed to JSONL, making the "left side" and "right
      side" of the 3-way merge BOTH visible in the JSONL and
      converting the test from a 3-way-merge reproduction into a
      straight fast-forward test.

  The comment above the test case is rewritten to capture the
  actual invariant: "relation changes (labels, dependencies,
  comments) must participate in merge-change detection even when
  scalar issue content is unchanged on both sides."

src/storage/sqlite.rs — pure rustfmt:
  Unrelated: rustfmt reflowed a handful of `make_issue(...)` calls
  in the tests module from single-line to multi-line because the
  arg list now exceeds the line-width threshold after an unrelated
  edit landed nearby. Also reflowed one
  `storage.update_issue(...).unwrap()` chain. No behavior change.
  Committed alongside the repro test fix so bench runs stop
  re-creating the diff; no rustfmt-before-commit hook exists yet.
